{E_MAIN_WALL_REPAIR}::{WALL_FINISHES_TYP: The external surfaces of the walls or parts of it have been painted. Painting of external wall surfaces can increase the risk of dampness and frost damage. This is because rainwater can find its way through tiny cracks in the paint to saturate the wall but it is then prevented from freely evaporating into the atmosphere by the paint film, which acts as a cloak to entrapped moisture. High levels of internal humidity can also lead to a build-up of entrapped moisture in painted external walls, increasing the risk of spalling in the brickwork. As the external surfaces have been painted, this will be an ongoing decoration/maintenance burden in the future.

{F_CEILINGS}::{IF_LATH_AND_PLASTER_IS_SE:
Some or all the ceilings are made of older plaster supported on thin wooden strips (called ‘lath and plaster’). Under normal use, older ‘lath and plaster ceiling’ (usually before the 1940s), can become unstable when the layer of plaster becomes detached from the laths beneath. Although I could see no problems now, you should expect more repairs in the future especially when you redecorate.
\r\n
\r\nThe lath and plaster ceilings will require periodic attention and repair due to their age and condition. They are particularly susceptible to vibration and disturbance, and localised re-plastering repairs can be expected particularly during redecoration work. Lath and plaster ceilings are difficult to repair as large areas can be dislodged by the repair work itself. In some cases, it may be economical to replace the whole ceiling.

{F_WALLS_AND_PARTITIONS}::{IF_LATH_AND_P:
Some or all the walls are made of older plaster supported on thin wooden strips (called ‘lath and plaster’). Under normal use, older ‘lath and plaster ceiling’ (usually before the 1940s), can become unstable when the layer of plaster becomes detached from the laths beneath. Although I could see no problems now, you should expect more repairs in the future especially when you redecorate.
\r\n
\r\nThe lath and plaster walls will require periodic attention and repair due to their age and condition. They are particularly susceptible to vibration and disturbance, and localized re-plastering repairs can be expected particularly during redecoration work, and this could be costly.

{K_VALUATION}::{K_VALUATION_PROPERTY_TYP:
K – Additional assumption

My opinion has been arrived at largely by the standard assumptions governing residential valuations. (These are outlined in this section and under market value on the description of the Home Buyer service). However, one additional assumption has been made: this is that the property has a Lease with an unexpired term of approximately 90 years.

K - Other Considerations

The Lease still has a considerable term to run, and no action needs to be taken immediately. The value of leasehold properties will diminish as the lease gets progressively shorter. Once the lease is 80 years or less; it will have a significant impact on the valuation of the property, and you should, therefore, consider extending the lease under the terms of the Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993 well before this stage.
